title: GlobalChip Announces Rs 28,000 Crore Semiconductor ATMP Plant in Gujarat; 5,000 Jobs Expected
content:
GlobalChip Technologies on Thursday announced a Rs 28,000 crore investment to build an OSAT/ATMP semiconductor facility in Gujarat, marking one of the largest private investments in India's electronics manufacturing ecosystem this year. Construction is expected to begin in Q3 2026, with first commercial output targeted for late 2028.

The company said the plant will focus on packaging and testing for automotive chips, power management ICs, and industrial microcontrollers. At peak capacity, the facility is projected to process up to 18 million units per day across multiple packaging lines.

Union IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said the project aligns with India's semiconductor roadmap and will be supported through approved incentives under the modified semiconductor scheme. State officials confirmed that land allocation and utility clearances have been completed for the first phase.

GlobalChip expects the project to create around 5,000 direct jobs and 13,000 indirect jobs across logistics, ancillary materials, and equipment services. The company also announced partnerships with two Indian engineering institutes for workforce training in chip packaging, reliability testing, and process control.

Industry experts said the ATMP segment is strategically important because it shortens supply chains and improves resilience for domestic electronics and EV manufacturers. However, they cautioned that execution risk remains high due to long equipment lead times and the need for stable power and ultra-pure water systems.

Shares of listed electronics manufacturing companies gained between 1.8% and 4.6% after the announcement as investors priced in potential order opportunities for local suppliers.
author: Anirban Sen
